title: Creamy Portuguese Tofu Stir-Fry
description: Savory, creamy, and energizing vegan tofu stir-fry infused with Portuguese spices and a splash of rum.
instructions: Press tofu for 15 minutes to remove excess water, then cut into 1-inch cubes and pat dry with paper towels.
Heat a large wok or skillet over high heat until shimmering. Add 1 tbsp olive oil, then add tofu cubes. Stir-fry tofu until golden on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove tofu and set aside.
In the same wok, add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil. Add minced garlic and sliced onion, sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add sliced red bell pepper and cherry tomatoes, stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until vegetables start to soften.
Return tofu to the wok. Add coconut milk, soy sauce, ground cumin, and smoked paprika. Stir well to combine.
Pour in rum and let the mixture simmer for 3-4 minutes at medium heat, allowing flavors to meld.
Stir in lime juice, taste, and adjust seasoning if needed. Cook for another 1–2 minutes until sauce slightly thickens.
Remove from heat. Plate the stir-fry over steamed rice or quinoa if desired, and garnish with chopped cilantro (optional).
Serve hot and enjoy the creamy Portuguese-inspired tofu dish.
